children.name: Rhagologus
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Mottled Berryhunter, a recently recognised family-level lineage endemic to the montane forests of New Guinea. It was historically placed with the whistlers or woodswallows but molecular studies revealed it is sufficiently distinct to warrant its own family. It is a medium-sized passerine with a stout, thrush-like build and a heavy bill adapted for feeding on fruit and large insects.

children.name: Rhagologus leucostigma
children.description: A medium-sized, thrush-like bird with mottled brown, grey, and white plumage that provides exceptional camouflage in the forest gloom, found in the montane forests of New Guinea. Its stout, heavy bill is adapted for crushing berries, seeds, and large insects. It is a shy, secretive bird of the mid-storey, more often heard than seen, with a simple, melancholic whistle.
